title: Positional Number Representation and Bases
summary: Covers the positional representation of numbers, the concept of base (radix), why computers use base 2, and the representable limits in finite bit widths.
content_points: Value = Σ a_i * d^i; d = base; a_i = digit at position i.
Computers use d=2: only two digits (0,1); easiest hardware implementation; matches Boole logic.
For n integer digits and m fractional digits: overflow if > 2^n - 1; approximation error ≤ 2^(-m).

formulas.name: Positional value
formulas.expression: N = Σ a_i * d^i  for i from -m to n-1

learning_objectives: Explain why base 2 is used in computers
Determine the range and precision of an unsigned integer representation
